items.name: Big Five Africa Origin Concept Conservation Safari Marketing
items.notes: Term coined by big-game hunters for the five most dangerous to hunt — lion, leopard, elephant, rhino, buffalo. Conservation irony: now top-five photographed. Combined current Serengeti/Masai Mara population estimates for all five

items.name: African Cheetah Population East Africa Hunting Success Rate Conservation Threats
items.notes: Deeper facts: ~7,100 left globally, East Africa subpopulation, 50% hunting success rate, cub mortality from lions (up to 90%), IUCN Vulnerable, conflict with livestock farmers

items.name: East Africa Conservation Protected Area Coverage Kenya Tanzania Rwanda Facts
items.notes: % land under protection in Tanzania (38%), Kenya (12%), Rwanda (10%), total ecosystem corridors, wildlife corridor threats, IUCN managed area categories, what conservation fees pay for

items.name: Zanzibar Stone Town Luxury Hotels Park Hyatt Emerson Spice Serena Inn

items.notes: Kim's content — Cai Rang floating market, luxury river cruises (Bassac, Victoria Mekong), best season (Dec–Apr), Can Tho as base, homestay vs boutique lodge, bird sanctuary

items.name: Spotted Hyena Serengeti Clan Structure Matriarchal Predator Scavenger Facts
items.notes: Crocuta crocuta — largest clan sizes, matriarchal society, more efficient predator than lions (75% successful hunts), Serengeti population est. 7,000–10,000, nocturnal vs diurnal activity

items.name: Masai Giraffe Serengeti Masai Mara Population IUCN Conservation Status
items.notes: Giraffa camelopardalis tippelskirchi — IUCN Vulnerable since 2016, Serengeti population 13,000+, tallest land animal (5.5–6m), silent extinction (30% decline in 30 years), ossicones fact
